text: The MultiMahjong system shall consist of a MultiMahjongServer and a MultiMahjongClient program.
The MultiMahjong system shall allow up to 4 players to play Mahjong over a TCP/IP network.
The MultiMahjongClient program shall allow 1 player to play in a stand-alone mode.
The MultiMahjong system shall provide computer opponents to ensure every game has 4 players.
The MultiMahjongServer shall reside on a central computer connected to a TCP/IP network with a unique IP address.
The MultiMahjongServer shall allow and communicate with connections from MultiMahjongClients using IP.
The MultiMahjongServer shall relay data received from one MultiMahjongClient to other requesting MultiMahjongClients.
The MultiMahjongClient shall allow the user to create a new multi-player game.
The MultiMahjongClient shall allow the user to create a new single-player game.
The MultiMahjongClient shall allow the user to join an existing multi-player game.
The MultiMahjongClient shall allow the user to quit the program when no game is in progress.
The MultiMahjongClient shall require the user to enter a name and choose an icon when creating or joining a game.
The MultiMahjongClient shall limit the total number of players to 4, including human and computer opponents.
The MultiMahjongClient shall allow the user to set the score limit for winning a hand.
The MultiMahjongClient shall send game initialization and join information to the MultiMahjongServer.
The MultiMahjongClient shall perform the processing for any required Computer Opponents.
The MultiMahjongClient shall retrieve a list of available games from the MultiMahjongServer.
The MultiMahjongClient shall retrieve player names and seating positions from the MultiMahjongServer.
The MultiMahjongClient shall allow moves only according to the Chinese rules of Mahjong.
The MultiMahjongClient shall inform the user when it is their turn to pick up a tile.
The MultiMahjongClient shall display when another player picks up a tile from the wall.
The MultiMahjongClient shall display when another player exposes a set of tiles.
The MultiMahjongClient shall display when another player discards a tile.
The MultiMahjongClient shall inform the user when they are eligible to Chow, Pung, Kong, or Mahjong.
The MultiMahjongClient shall allow the user to pick up a tile from the wall if it is their turn.
The MultiMahjongClient shall allow the user to discard a tile after picking one up.
The MultiMahjongClient shall inform the user if another player is fishing for Mahjong.
The MultiMahjongClient shall inform the user if another player goes Mahjong.
The MultiMahjongClient shall inform the user if the hand ends in a draw.
The game shall end when all 4 rounds have been played or if the user chooses to end the game.
The MultiMahjongServer shall inform all connected MultiMahjongClients when a game has ended.
The Computer Opponent shall not have access to concealed tiles in other players' hands or tiles in the wall.
The Computer Opponent shall have access to the number of tiles remaining in the wall and in players' hands.
The Computer Opponent shall be able to read all exposed or revealed hands.
The Computer Opponent shall be able to pick up tiles, discard tiles, reveal Kongs, and perform Chow, Pung, or Mahjong actions.
The MultiMahjongClient shall provide a dialogue box reporting the nature of the error when a nonfatal error occurs.
The MultiMahjongClient shall allow the user to continue the current game after a nonfatal error.
The MultiMahjongClient shall display a dialogue box and terminate execution when a fatal error occurs.
The MultiMahjong system shall be written using JDK 1.2.
The MultiMahjong system shall follow the Sun Microsystems Java coding standard.
The MultiMahjong software shall run on machines capable of running a Java Virtual Machine supporting JDK 1.2.
The Computer Opponent shall respond within 1 minute in both single and multi-player modes.
The MultiMahjongServer shall handle requests for up to 10 simultaneous games.
The MultiMahjongClient shall calculate possible Chow, Pung, Kong, or Mahjong moves within 5 seconds of a discard.
The MultiMahjongServer shall be operated via a command prompt without a graphical user interface.
The MultiMahjongClient shall provide a graphical user interface interactable via mouse and keyboard.
The MultiMahjongClient GUI shall be contained within a screen resolution of 800 x 600 pixels.
The MultiMahjongClient GUI shall display all graphics in 16-bit color.
The MultiMahjongClient Main Window shall display the user's tiles and the tile wall.
The MultiMahjongClient Main Window shall display buttons for game actions and grey them out when inactive.
The MultiMahjong product shall include an administrator's manual for the server in electronic format.
The MultiMahjong product shall include a user's manual for the client in both hard copy and electronic format.
